# Deep bug audit — Aria high-risk subsystems You are auditing **Aria**, an iOS 16.6 music app (SwiftUI, Swift 5, zero third-party deps) with a FastAPI + yt-dlp backend. Repo root is the `Aria_Music_Browser/` Xcode project; backend is in `backend/`. Project-wide `SWIFT_DEFAULT_ACTOR_ISOLATION = MainActor`. Hunt for **real, currently-present bugs** in the three subsystems below. This codebase already passed an 85-finding audit (≈48 fixed); do NOT re-report known/fixed items or style nits. I want
Is there a way I can ssh into a remote machine from claude desktop?
Could you create a prompt that I could send to claude on another pc to transfer all skills and mcps I have in MuiscAppIOS on this pc onto my other machine's home directory.
When two things try to save the user's library at the same instant, the file gets truncated and people permanently lose their playlists.
Downloaded songs are ending up world-readable in the shared container, so other apps on the phone can read the user's whole library; lock the file permissions down.
Wire the new low-level audio-mixing engine into the streaming playback path so the equalizer and crossfade run on one shared signal chain.
Playback intermittently stalls when the network flaps mid-song; work out why the buffering coordinator gets stuck and rework it so it cannot.
Rework how the play queue and the now-playing screen stay consistent when the user reorders songs during playback, because the state drifts and the UI glitches.
Show elapsed and remaining time on either side of the mini-player scrubber.
When a track download fails, surface a retry affordance to the user instead of failing silently.
Remember the user's last-selected equalizer preset between app launches.
What does the project-wide MainActor default setting actually change about how code runs?
Give the now-playing screen a slightly warmer background tint.
